title: I turned down $500K at FAANG to stay indie. No regrets.
post:
I had an offer: $500K total compensation at a FAANG company. I turned it down.

Not because I'm principled or wealthy. Because I did the math.

$500K sounds life-changing until you realize it's $180K base and the rest is stock that vests over four years. You'd be trading your 20s for a golden handcuff.

I chose to stay indie. Build products, write code, own my time. Three years later, I make less money. But I'm happier, I've built real things, and I never once answered to a product manager.

The tech industry has convinced everyone that FAANG is the finish line. It's not. It's a trade: your autonomy for their brand.

Some people make that trade consciously. That's fine. But most take it without asking if there's another option.

I'm not special. I just decided the cost of the golden handcuff was too high.

Would you take the FAANG offer? Thoughts?
